content.stem: In a 3×3 grid, each row and column follows a pattern. Row 1: circle, square, triangle. Row 2: square, triangle, circle. What completes Row 3: triangle, ___, square?
content.explanation: Each row contains the same three shapes in different orders, and each column also contains all three shapes. Column 2 has square, triangle, so it needs circle to complete the set.

metadata.cognitive_skill: simultaneous relational reasoning across two dimensions
metadata.misconception_targeted: focusing on single dimension rather than matrix constraints

distractor_rationale.reasoning: Student thinks the missing shape should repeat the first shape in the row
Student copies the pattern from the cell directly above or focuses only on row completion
Student thinks a new shape is needed rather than understanding the constraint of using only the given shapes
